name: Markets in Financial Instruments Directive II (MiFID II)
summary: MiFID II is the EU's comprehensive framework for regulating financial markets, investment services, and investor protection. It replaced MiFID I with significantly expanded scope, introducing stricter transparency rules, best execution requirements, product governance obligations, and new trading venue categories to strengthen market integrity across the European Union.
keyRequirements: Pre-trade and post-trade transparency requirements for equities, bonds, derivatives, and structured finance products
Best execution obligations with detailed policies, monitoring, and annual reporting to clients
Product governance requirements: manufacturers must define target markets and distribution strategies
Inducements ban for independent advisors; enhanced disclosure for non-independent advice
Transaction reporting to national competent authorities with 65+ data fields within T+1
Algorithmic and high-frequency trading controls including kill switches and risk limits
Third-country firm access through equivalence decisions or member state national regimes
penalties: National competent authorities may impose fines of at least EUR 5 million for natural persons and EUR 5 million or 10% of annual turnover for legal persons. Additional sanctions include public censure, withdrawal of authorization, temporary bans on management functions, and disgorgement of profits.
